domain: origami Miura-ori (tessellated degree-4-vertex crease pattern with alternating mountain/valley folds satisfying Maekawa + Kawasaki theorems; rigid panels, single deployment DOF, flat-foldable, negative in-plane Poisson, scale-independent kinematics)
specific_mechanism: A flat sheet tessellated into rigid parallelogram panels connected by creases that obey local rules (Maekawa |M-V|=2, Kawasaki alternate-angle sum=π). The whole sheet folds/unfolds along ONE scalar parameter; panels never deform.
llm_application: Miura-tessellated parameter manifold (MTPM): organize each weight matrix as a tessellation of rigid low-rank panels connected by learnable rotation-style mixers satisfying signed-mixing balance (Maekawa-equivalent) and alternate-subspace-alignment constraints (Kawasaki-equivalent). Single scalar deployment parameter θ smoothly slides between expanded training tensor and collapsed deployment tensor, lossless by construction. No PCA discarding; structural folding preserves all components.
novelty_claim: FLAT-LLM (2505.23966) does head-wise PCA truncation. DFloat11 (2504.11651) is byte-level lossless. ZipServ (2603.17435) is hardware-aware byte lossless. None impose a single-DOF kinematic Maekawa+Kawasaki constraint on the parameter manifold. Lossless-by-construction single-scalar deploy θ via tessellated rigid panels is not retrieved.
